NileCare Group Health Policy
Policy No. NC-GH-2026-0417  |  Terms & Conditions  |  Effective 01 January 2026
1. Scope of Cover
This policy provides medical cover to the insured member and enrolled dependents for treatment
received at facilities within the NileCare provider network. The maximum annual coverage ceiling is
EGP 500,000 per member per policy year. Cover operates on a cashless basis at in-network
hospitals and on a reimbursement basis elsewhere, subject to the limits set out in Section 4.
2. Waiting Periods
A general waiting period of 30 days applies from the enrolment date, during which only accidental
emergencies are covered. Pre-existing conditions are subject to a waiting period of 12 months.

Benefit
Annual Limit (EGP)
Co-pay
Notes
Inpatient / Hospitalization
500,000
0%

Outpatient (consult + diagnostics)
30,000

40,000

3,000
20%
Excludes cosmetic
Optical
1,500
0%
Once every 2 years
5. Claims and Reimbursement
For treatment received outside the network, claims must be submitted within 60 days of the date of
service, accompanied by original invoices and the treating physician's report. Approved
reimbursements are settled within 15 working days of receipt of a complete claim file. Incomplete
files are returned to the member and the 15-day period restarts on resubmission.

emergencies are covered. Pre-existing conditions are subject to a waiting period of 12 months.
Maternity benefits become available after a continuous waiting period of 10 months. No waiting
period applies to accidental injury.
3. Co-payment and Deductibles
Inpatient treatment at in-network hospitals carries no co-payment. Outpatient consultations and
diagnostics are subject to a 10% co-payment payable by the member at the point of service.
Dental treatment carries a 20% co-payment. Where a member elects a room category higher than
the entitled private room, the difference in cost and the resulting proportional increase in
associated charges are borne by the member.
4. Benefit Limits
Benefit
Annual Limit (EGP)
Co-pay
Notes
Inpatient / Hospitalization
500,000
0%
Private room entitlement

NileCare — FAQ / ■■■■■■■ ■■■■■■■
Q: How do I add a newborn dependent?
Submit the birth certificate through the member
portal within 30 days of birth. Cover for the newborn
begins from the date of birth once enrolment is
approved.
Q: Is a referral needed for a specialist?
No. Outpatient specialist consultations do not require
a referral, but the 10% co-payment applies at the
point of service.
Q: What is the annual outpatient limit?
Outpatient consultations and diagnostics are covered
up to EGP 30,000 per member per year, separate
from the inpatient ceiling.
Q: How long do reimbursements take?
Complete out-of-network claims are settled within 15
working days. Claims must be filed within 60 days of
the date of service.
Q: Are chronic medications covered during the
waiting period?
per_question.response: The maximum annual coverage ceiling per member is EGP 500,000 [source:01_policy_terms_en.pdf:0].
per_question.reference: EGP 500,000 per member per policy year.

Claims Procedure Guide
Reimbursement workflow for out-of-network treatment
Step 1 — Obtain documentation
Collect the original itemized invoice, the treating physician's report, and any diagnostic results.
Photocopies are not accepted for out-of-network reimbursement.
Step 2 — Submit within the deadline
Upload documents through the member portal within 60 days of the date of service. Late
submissions are rejected except in documented cases of hospitalization exceeding 30 days.
Step 3 — Assessment
NileCare reviews the file and applies the relevant benefit limit and co-payment. The 10% outpatient
co-payment or 20% dental co-payment is deducted from the approved amount before settlement.
Step 4 — Settlement
per_question.response: A 10% co-payment applies to outpatient consultations [source:01_policy_terms_en.pdf:0].
per_question.reference: A 10% co-payment, payable by the member at the point of service.

No. Outpatient specialist consultations do not require
a referral, but the 10% co-payment applies at the
point of service.
Q: What is the annual outpatient limit?
Outpatient consultations and diagnostics are covered
up to EGP 30,000 per member per year, separate
from the inpatient ceiling.
Q: How long do reimbursements take?
Complete out-of-network claims are settled within 15
working days. Claims must be filed within 60 days of
the date of service.
Q: Are chronic medications covered during the
waiting period?

5. Claims and Reimbursement
For treatment received outside the network, claims must be submitted within 60 days of the date of
service, accompanied by original invoices and the treating physician's report. Approved
reimbursements are settled within 15 working days of receipt of a complete claim file. Incomplete
files are returned to the member and the 15-day period restarts on resubmission.

Claims Procedure Guide
Reimbursement workflow for out-of-network treatment
Step 1 — Obtain documentation
Collect the original itemized invoice, the treating physician's report, and any diagnostic results.
Photocopies are not accepted for out-of-network reimbursement.
Step 2 — Submit within the deadline
Upload documents through the member portal within 60 days of the date of service. Late
submissions are rejected except in documented cases of hospitalization exceeding 30 days.
Step 3 — Assessment
NileCare reviews the file and applies the relevant benefit limit and co-payment. The 10% outpatient
co-payment or 20% dental co-payment is deducted from the approved amount before settlement.
Step 4 — Settlement

co-payment or 20% dental co-payment is deducted from the approved amount before settlement.
Step 4 — Settlement
Approved amounts are transferred to the member's registered bank account within 15 working
days of a complete submission.

per_question.response: A 10% co-payment applies to outpatient consultations and diagnostics, payable by the member at the point of service [source:01_policy_terms_en.pdf:0].
